rulings.Case Title: DARYL BERKSON BRAND VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Continued) Motion To Compel Further Responses To Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents And Things
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, JUNE 26, 2012, LINE 5, PLAINTIFFS DARYL BERKSON BRAND, THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM BRAND'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION AND INSPECTION OF DOCUMENTS AND THINGS. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. No tentative; parties to appear. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WILSON POWELL et al VS. DOUGLAS R. LIVINGSTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 26, 2012, Line 3. DEFENDANTS DOUGLAS LIVINGSTON AND VAN DE POEL, LEVY & ALLEN LLP'S Motion For Good Faith Settlement. Granted. The settling parties made a sufficient showing of the basis for the settlement and the allocations and Lexington did not satisfy its burden of showing that either the settlement or the allocations are so far outside the Tech-Bilt ballpark to lack good faith. The parties are reminded that the allocations are not binding on non-settling parties. (Weil & Brown at 12:932). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: LOUIS GAMACHE et al VS. NICK VOLENTE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andm Motion In Consolidated Matter For Reconsideration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 26, 2012, Line 29. PLAINTIFFS LOUIS GAMACHE, PAUL GARRARD AND DANIELLE MCGEE'S Notice Of Motion and Motion In Consolidated Matter For Reconsideration. Granted. The May 10, 2012 order sustaining the demurrer without leave to amend is vacated. Plaintiff has leave to file the proposed Second Amended Complaint by June 28, 2012. Plaintiff shall pay $500 to defendant to cover defendant's costs for appearing at the prior demurrer hearing and preparing the order sustaining the demurrer without leave to amend. That payment is to be made by July 13, 2012. Code of Civil Procedure 473 is a sufficient basis for the relief sought. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SALMA KHAN MD VS. HEALTH SYSTEMS DEPARTMENT, SAN MATEO COUNTY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents (Part 2)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 26, 2012, Line 30. Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents (Part 2). (7) This ruling does not preclude the parties from arguing at the time of trial that the disclosed records are not relevant and thus, inadmissible. Nor does this ruling necessarily preclude plaintiff from later seeking additional information concerning any of these 33 patients by other appropriate means in accordance with the California Code of Civil Procedure, at which time any party may object in the usual course of discovery. Granted as to Request nos. 50 and 51 as Defendants shall produce all North County schedules (the one-page sheet that lists the daily schedule for all physicians and clinicians at the Clinic) for the period between December 9, 2008 and June 9, 2009. Granted as to Request no. 58 as defendants shall produce all business or medical licenses required to operate a mental health clinic. Granted, in part, as to Request no. 59 as the request is overbroad and unduly burdensome. Defendants have confirmed that that there have been no other lawsuits filed by a physician or resident alleging discrimination under the DFEH/FEHA or the ADA in the past ten (10) years. Defendants shall provide a supplemental request verifying that information. Denied as to Request no. 60 and both parties' requests for monetary sanctions. Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 2031.010, to the extent defendants' supplemental response sets forth an inability to comply with the request, the demand shall state thata diligent search and reasonable inquiry has been made in an effort to locate the item demanded and set forth the reason defendants are unable to comply (e.g. the document never existed; has been lost or stolen; has been destroyed; or is not in the possession, custody or control of defendants.) With the exception of the medical records addressed in Request Numbers 15 and 86, defendants are HEREBY ORDERED to produce further responses complying with this order within thirty (30) days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SALMA KHAN MD VS. HEALTH SYSTEMS DEPARTMENT, SAN MATEO COUNTY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 26, 2012, Line 30. Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions. The court adopts in full the recommendation of Pro Tem Thornley as follows: Granted as to Requests 12, 14, 18, 22, 26, 35, 39, 48, 49, 56 and 105. Granted in part as to Request 33 as the term "relating to" is overbroad and, based on their representation, defendants shall serve a supplemental verified response that there are no written policies that required plaintiff to attend weekly meetings. Granted in part as to Requests 15 and 86 as follows: (1) Within five (5) days from the date of this Order, defendants shall provide plaintiff with a complete list of all patients plaintiff treated while in the employ of the North County Clinic; (2) Within ten (10) days of receiving the patient list, plaintiff shall provide a list of twenty-two (22) of her former patients at the North County Clinic to defendants; (3) Within fifteen (15) days of receiving plaintiff's list, defendants shall make available to plaintiff the mental health records documented in the period of August 5, 2002 through June 9, 2009 of (a) those 22 patients on plaintiff's list and (b) the 11 patients whose records George Wilkinson, M.D. relied upon, as set forth in the motion and opposition papers. The records for these 33 patients shall be made available at a County facility other than the North County Mental Health Clinic. The County may have an employee present to ensure the records are not tampered with; (4) Plaintiff will have one (1) single day, from 9:00 a.m. through 5:00 p.m. (with an hour break for lunch), to review and flag records that she would like to copy. Her copy service will have one (1) day to copy the records. In the event it takes more than one (1) day for the copy service to copy the records, plaintiff's copy service will be provided additional time to copy the records, but plaintiff must reimburse the County for the cost of having its employee present to monitor the records for the additional time; (5) The review and copy of the records must be completed within thirty (30) days from the date defendants made available the records, unless otherwise stipulated in writing by the parties or on ex parte order of the court on showing of good cause and after good faith "meet and confer" on the issue; (6) Release of the patient information hereunder shall be governed by the parties' stipulated protective order; = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ADELAIDA USI et al VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 26, 2012, Line 10. DEFENDANT P.G.H.C.C., INC. DBA PACIFIC GROVE CONVALESCENT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. PGHCC did not satisfy its burden of production. Plaintiffs' objections to the declaration of Ms. Mulvey are sustained. The declaration does not show that it was made on personal knowledge and does not sufficiently identify the records. Plaintiffs' objections to the declaration of Ms. Ingram are sustained. The medical records that Ms. Ingram relies on are not properly before the court. AND VIRAGE LOGIC CORPORATION'S Notice Of Motion To File Under Seal Portions Of Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award. Granted. Respondent filed a statement of non-opposition and the motion complies with the applicable rules of court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted by the moving party without need for the moving party to appear. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RICCI CORNELL et al VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Set Aside Dismissal
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 26, 2012, Line 19. PLAINTIFFS RICCI CORNELL, CLARICE LACAU, CORZAC, INC., DBA JELLY'S, AND DANCE CAFE'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Dismissal. Denied. The Court did not lack jurisdiction to enter the order dismissing this action. judgment of dismissal and thus the order is not void but voidable. Plaintiff failed to act to void the order before it became final, thus preventing this Court from granting the relief requested by Plaintiff. Even if the Court could grant such relief, there is no good cause to do so because a plain reading of the Complaint shows that the request for injunctive relief is incidental to the prayer for damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN MOORE VS. C & S WHOLESALE GROCERS INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Approve Prop 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 26, 2012, Line 20. PLAINTIFF JOHN MOORE'S Notice Of Motion To Approve Prop 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment. Granted. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with Health and Safety Code section 25249.7. While the court has some concerns about the reasonableness of the fees agreed to by the parties and the sufficiency of the evidentiary showing made by plaintiff for approval of the fees, unless and until the court has developed a better system of evaluating the reasonableness of fees agreed to by the parties in Proposition 65 cases where there is no opposition made to those fees, the court is satisfied that the showing made here is adequate, although perhaps barely so. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CITIBANK, N.A. VS. LATOSHA A HUBBARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Order Deeming Pltfs Request For Admissions
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, JUNE 26, 2012, LINE 3, PLAINTIFF CITIBANK, N.A.'S MOTION FOR ORDER DEEMING PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied without prejudice. The moving party failed to provide courtesy copies of the moving papers in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)
